chapters.title: Early Life
chapters.subtitle: Birth to age 12
chapters.description: The world you were born into. The people who shaped your first years.

questions.text: Where were you born, and what year?
Who raised you? Who was in your household growing up?
Where did you grow up — describe the place.
What's the dominant feeling you associate with your childhood?
Who made you feel safest as a child?
Close your eyes — you're a kid again. Describe one scene: what do you see, hear, smell?
A specific room, smell, or sound from home that you still remember.
What did your childhood teach you about the world, even if you didn't know it then?
Is there anything from those early years you're still carrying today?

chapters.title: Adolescence
chapters.subtitle: Ages 13 to 18
chapters.description: The years you started figuring out who you were. The good parts and the hard parts.

questions.text: Where were you living during your teenage years?
Who were your people — friends, a crew, a scene?
What were you into? Music, sports, art, trouble?
What feeling defined your teenage years most — freedom, pressure, loneliness, love?
Was there a moment in your teens that cracked something open in you?
A specific place from your teens you can still picture perfectly. Describe it.
A song, smell, or image that takes you straight back to that time.
What did you believe about yourself at 17 that turned out to be wrong?
What did those years give you that you still use?

chapters.title: Leaving Home
chapters.subtitle: Your first moves into the world
chapters.description: The moment you stepped out on your own — what you were chasing, what you were leaving.

questions.text: How old were you when you first left home, and where did you go?
What were the circumstances — college, work, escape, adventure?
What did leaving feel like? Relief, fear, excitement, grief?
Was there something you were running toward, or running from?
Your first place you lived on your own — describe it.
A specific moment from that early independent period that's stuck with you.
What did you learn about yourself the first time you were truly on your own?
Did you become who you expected to become?

chapters.title: Work & Career
chapters.subtitle: How you spent your time and built your life
chapters.description: The jobs, the grinds, the callings, the pivots. What work meant to you.

questions.text: What did you do for work over the years? Walk me through the main chapters.
Was there a job or career that defined a big chunk of your life?
What work made you feel most alive — most like yourself?
Was there work you did purely to survive, that cost you something?
Describe a specific place where you worked — what it looked, sounded, felt like.
A day of work you remember vividly, good or bad.
What does work mean to you — a means to an end, an identity, a calling?
What are you most proud of that you built or accomplished?

chapters.title: Love & Relationships
chapters.subtitle: The people who got close
chapters.description: Who you loved, who loved you, what love taught you.

questions.text: Tell me about a significant relationship — how did it start?
Have you been married, partnered, or had a long relationship you'd want in this story?
What's the deepest you've ever felt for another person?
What's the hardest a relationship has hurt you?
A specific moment with someone you loved — describe it like a scene.
What do you associate with that person — a sound, smell, image?
What has love taught you?
What do you wish you'd known about love when you were younger?

chapters.title: Family You Built
chapters.subtitle: The people you chose and created
chapters.description: Children, chosen family, deep friendships. The tribe you made.

questions.text: Do you have children? Tell me about them.
Who is in your family now — the people you count as yours?
What does it feel like to have built something with people?
Is there a moment with your family that you return to when you need strength?
A family scene — a meal, a trip, a morning — describe it.
What do you want to pass down to the people who come after you?
What kind of parent, partner, or friend have you tried to be?

chapters.title: Hard Times
chapters.subtitle: When things broke — and how you survived
chapters.description: The losses, the failures, the dark chapters. What they cost you. What they made you.

questions.text: What's the hardest thing you've been through?
Was there a period where everything seemed to fall apart at once?
What did rock bottom feel like for you?
What got you through it — or who?
A specific moment from a hard chapter — what do you see when you go back there?
What did hard times take from you that you never got back?
What did they give you that you couldn't have gotten any other way?

chapters.title: What You're Proud Of
chapters.subtitle: The wins, the breakthroughs, the things you built
chapters.description: Not just trophies. The moments you knew you'd done something real.

questions.text: What's something you did that you're genuinely proud of?
Was there a goal you chased for a long time that you finally reached?
What did it feel like in the moment you realized you'd done it?
Did anyone see you do it? Did that matter?
Describe a proud moment — where were you, what was around you?
What does that achievement say about who you are?
What do you want people to know you built or stood for?

chapters.title: Who You Are Now
chapters.subtitle: Present day
chapters.description: Where you are. What you've figured out. What still surprises you about yourself.

questions.text: Where are you living now, and what does your day-to-day look like?
What are you working on or building right now?
How do you feel about where you are in life today?
What still excites you? What still scares you?
Describe a typical good day in your current life.
What do you understand now that you didn't at 30? At 20?
What do you still want?

chapters.title: Your Legacy
chapters.subtitle: What you want to leave behind
chapters.description: Not what you owned. What you meant. What you want the people after you to know.

questions.text: What do you want people to know about your life when you're gone?
Is there a message you've always wanted to pass on?
What do you most want to be remembered for?
Is there anything you want to make right, say, or leave clear?
If someone could see one scene from your life to understand you — what would it be?
What was your life about? In a sentence or two.
What do you want the people who love you to carry forward?
